artPrompt: A sweeping widescreen film-still looking down a town green as an immense column of ordinary neighbors marches shoulder to shoulder toward the viewer, the foreground figures sharp and resolute and the ranks dissolving into golden haze far behind, picket fences and folding tables swallowed by the swelling tide of people; epic scale, warm afternoon backlight rimming a thousand silhouettes, rendered as gritty cinematic digital concept art — highly detailed, dramatic photoreal lighting, atmospheric haze, realistic textures.
name: Strength in Numbers
text: Until end of turn, creatures you control get +X/+X, where X is the number of creatures you control minus the greatest number of creatures any single opponent controls. (If that number is negative, the movement is smaller than the machine, and X is 0. Keep organizing.)
flavorText: One neighbor is a complaint. A hundred neighbors are a recall. A thousand neighbors are a parking problem AND a recall.

extras.frontrunnerPunish: scales vs the biggest opponent board
